name: Gable end wall
description: Triangular wall section that closes the short side of a gabled roof, rising from the eaves line to the ridge apex. Often treated as the most ornamented facade of a building: a decorative bargeboard traces the rake of the roof, an oeil-de-boeuf or louvred attic window pierces the upper triangle, and the lower rectangle commonly carries half-timber framing or carved corbels. Used as the front gable of medieval cottages, Tudor townhouses and Gothic chapels, where the steep apex doubles as a visual focal point and a ventilation point for the loft behind. The architecture_planner places one of these per gable end, aligning the apex with the ridge plotted by the silhouette skill.
